content.text: Generics let you write components — functions, classes, interfaces — that work with many types while keeping full type safety. Instead of writing one version per type or falling back to any, you define a type parameter that TypeScript resolves at each use site. The resolved type flows through the type system, so callers get correct autocomplete, error checking, and type inference without any extra effort.

attrs.alt: Side-by-side comparison: code duplication or losing type safety without generics vs type-safe reuse with generics

content.text: TypeScript supports generic type parameters on functions, classes, interfaces, and type aliases. Each placement follows the same angle-bracket syntax.

TypeScript infers the type argument from the concrete value passed to the function. Explicit type arguments are needed only when inference would produce a less specific type or when inference cannot proceed.
A constraint narrows what types may be used for a type parameter. The syntax is T extends Shape. Inside the function or class body, TypeScript allows access to all members of Shape on T.

The TypeScript standard library ships dozens of utility types that are all implemented with generic type parameters. Understanding how to write generics helps you understand how these utilities work and compose them correctly.
Generics in Angular at a Glance
a class implements or value is typed
a variable is declared with the type
A default type parameter provides a fallback type when the caller does not supply one. The syntax is T = DefaultType. Defaults are evaluated left to right and may reference earlier type parameters.

A type is covariant in T when it preserves the subtype relationship (if A extends B, then Foo<A> extends Foo<B>). A type is contravariant when the relationship is reversed. Understanding variance helps predict which generic types are assignable to each other.

TypeScript narrows generic types inside conditionals just as it does concrete types — but only when the type parameter is constrained enough that the compiler can determine which properties are available.
When TypeScript infers a type argument, it infers the widest type that satisfies the argument. This can produce a less specific type than intended.
Generic Utility Pattern Library — Common Building Blocks
These generic utilities appear repeatedly in Angular codebases. Recognising the pattern lets you read and write them fluently.
are reusable type-safe components parameterised by a type variable
